title: spec-bug: BootsToBasic isr-ran proxy fires on ROM pre-init at frame 20, real EI at frame 83
description: The M2.9 spec isr-ran proxy (mem.read(0x5C78) > 0) fires at frame 20 due to ROM pre-initializing FRAMES=2 during memory test — NOT real ISR. Real EI reached at frame 83. This causes frames-sustained to fail (loop runs frames 21-80, but FRAMES resets to 0 at frame 32; only recovers at frame 83). screen-non-empty also fails as screen written ~frame 350+, beyond 260-frame budget. Fix: detect FRAMES incrementing frame-over-frame, extend screen budget to 400 frames.
close_reason: Spec fixed: replaced FRAMES > 0 check with end-state snapshot at frame 500 (FRAMES >= 100 + monotonic incrementing check + screen-drawn). Defeats the frame-20 ROM-init false positive identified by sonnet subagent.

title: spec-bug: BootsToBasic ei-reached check uses cpu.iff1 post-runFrame which is always false
description: The BootsToBasic Suite spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-10-zx80-m2-9-boots-to-basic-design.md) defines the ei-reached check as 'if (machine.cpu.iff1)' after runFrame(). But runFrame() fires interruptRequest() at its end, which unconditionally sets cpu.iff1 = false when taking the INT. So iff1 is ALWAYS false at the point of the check. Observed: frames-counter=118 (ROM IS booting), screen-non-empty=true, but ei-reached=false and frames-incremented=false (because framesIncremented loop never runs since eiReached is false). Fix: detect ei-reached via mem.read(0x5C78) > 0 in the first loop, OR check mem.read(0x5C78) as the ei-proxy. Discovered during zx80-tmx implementation.
close_reason: Spec fixed: ei-reached check replaced with FRAMES counter detection (isr-ran). Subagent diagnosis was correct; iff1 is unreliable as a post-runFrame proxy because INT ack clears it.

title: Spec bug: Phase H test method names use colons (illegal in Kotlin backtick identifiers)
description: The DdFdPrefixPassthroughTest in the Phase H spec uses test method names containing colons (e.g. 'wrapping NOP: PC advances by 2, R bumped twice, T-states += 8'). This Kotlin version (1.x) rejects colons in backtick-quoted identifiers with 'Name contains illegal characters: :'. Fix: rename the test methods to use commas or other separators instead of colons. The test logic/assertions are unchanged. Discovered during H-A implementation.
close_reason: Bug noted by H-A subagent; the agent worked around by renaming test methods. Filing this here for future spec authors: NEVER use ':' in backtick-quoted Kotlin test method names — use ',' or other punctuation instead. Recurring pattern across M2.3, M2.5, and Phase H.

title: Plan M2.3: test names contain colons, fail to compile
description:
The implementation plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-10-zx80-m2-3-ula-renderer.md (Task 2 step 2.1) specifies three Kotlin test function names with embedded ':' colon characters:

  fun `flash off: bit 7 of attr is ignored when flashOn=false`()
  fun `flash on: bit 7 of attr inverts ink and paper when flashOn=true`()
  fun `bright bit: attribute 0x47 renders 0xFFFFFF not 0xCDCDCD`()

The Kotlin/JVM compiler rejects these with 'Name contains illegal characters: :.' because the colon is forbidden in JVM method names regardless of backtick escaping.

Suggested fix in the plan: replace ':' with ' -' or remove the colon, e.g. 'flash off bit 7 of attr is ignored when flashOn=false'. Test logic and intent unchanged.

Discovered while executing M2.3. Execution paused at step 2.2 per agent constraint: 'Do NOT deviate from the plan. If you find a plan bug, stop, file a beads issue, and report.'
close_reason: Fixed: ':' replaced with ',' in four test names

title: Plan bug: M2.2 EI/HALT runFrame test expects 69_888 exactly but INT adds +13
description: In docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-10-zx80-m2-2-frame-scheduler.md Step 4.1, test 'runFrame with EI HALT triggers INT and lands in ISR at 0x0038' asserts 'machine.cpu.tStates isEqualTo 69_888L'. But the implementation in Step 4.6 calls interruptRequest() at the end of runFrame(), which adds +13 T-states for IM 1 INT acknowledge. With the test's setup (EI executes, sets iff1=true, then HALT halts CPU; runFrame jumps tStates to 69_888 then fires INT → 69_901). Test fails with 'expected 69888, got 69901'. The spec at line 273 also has the same wording. Resolution: update assertion to isEqualTo(69_888L + 13) or isBetween(69_888L, 69_888L + 13), since INT has predictable timing on accept. Encountered while executing M2.2 plan.
close_reason: Fixed in plan and spec: assertion now isEqualTo(69_888L + 13L) with explanatory comment

title: M2.1 plan blocker: downloadRom URL returns 404 (redcode/ZX-Spectrum-48K-ROMs missing)
description: Plan 2026-05-10-zx80-m2-1-memory-rom-machine.md Step 3.1 specifies romUrl=https://github.com/redcode/ZX-Spectrum-48K-ROMs/raw/master/48.rom but that GitHub repo does not exist (api.github.com returns 404). Download fails on Step 3.2 with java.io.FileNotFoundException. Need a working source URL for the canonical Sinclair 48K ROM (16384 bytes). Candidates: amstrad.es archives, mame ROM dirs, or an Internet Archive mirror. Once URL is fixed, re-run plan from Task 3 Step 3.1.
close_reason: Fixed in plan: replaced 404 URL with rastersoft/fbzx mirror; SHA pre-pinned

title: M2.1 plan bug: downloadRom Kotlin DSL fails to compile java.net.URL / java.security.MessageDigest fully-qualified
description: The Task 3 plan code in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-10-zx80-m2-1-memory-rom-machine.md uses java.net.URL(romUrl) and java.security.MessageDigest.getInstance() as fully-qualified references in build.gradle.kts. Under Gradle 8.10 / Kotlin 1.9.24 DSL, this fails to compile with 'Unresolved reference: net' and 'Unresolved reference: security'. Minimum fix: add 'import java.net.URL' and 'import java.security.MessageDigest' at the top of build.gradle.kts (and use unqualified URL / MessageDigest in the body). Blocks M2.1-B (downloadRom) and therefore the rest of M2.1.
close_reason: Fixed in plan: imports added; downloadRom task now compiles

Sanity-check key FUSE cases: ed 44 (NEG), ed 47 (LD I,A), ed 57 (LD A,I), ed a0 (LDI), ed b0 (LDIR), ed b1 (CPIR), d3 (OUT), db (IN). Tag m1-phase02-10. title: WU 2.10-6: I/O — IN/OUT (18 opcodes via 12 classes)
description: 12 Op classes covering 18 opcodes: InAImm/OutImmA (main 0xDB/0xD3, port=(A shl 8)|n, R+=1, 11T), InRC parameterized (ED 40+8*r, port=cpu.bc, sets flags from byte read), OutCR parameterized (ED 41+8*r), InCFlags singleton (ED 70 — flags only, no register write), OutCZero singleton (ED 71 — writes 0). Block I/O: Ini/Ind/Inir/Indr (use cpu.io.read), Outi/Outd/Otir/Otdr (use cpu.io.write). Block I/O standard documented flags only (S/H/PV undocumented bits left at 0). Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~30 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-6. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-5: Block compare CPI/CPD/CPIR/CPDR (4 opcodes)
description: 4 singleton Op classes. Same shape as block move but compare A with mem[HL] instead of copying. Use Flags.afterSub(A, mem[HL], 0) for flag computation; do NOT update A (CP semantics). HL ± 1, BC -= 1. CPIR/CPDR exit when BC == 0 OR when match found (Z set). Loop iteration 21T; exit 16T. PC handling like LDIR. Flags: S/Z/H/N from afterSub; PV=(BC!=0 after); C preserved. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~14 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-5. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-4: Block move LDI/LDD/LDIR/LDDR (4 opcodes — exercises Trap B fix)
description: 4 singleton Op classes. LDI/LDD: one-shot block copy. LDIR/LDDR: looping variants — each execute() does ONE iteration; if BC != 0 leave PC unchanged (so next dispatch re-fires) and use 21T; if BC == 0 advance PC by 2 and use 16T. Trap B fix in FuseSuite drives the loop. Flags: H=0, N=0, P/V=(BC!=0 after); S/Z/C preserved. CRITICAL: Ldir test runs execute() twice manually to verify PC behavior at both points. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~16 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-4. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-2: Register transfers + NEG + RETI/RETN + RRD/RLD (8 opcodes)
description: 8 singleton Op classes: LdIA/LdRA (no flags), LdAI/LdAR (compute flags incl. P/V from iff2 — easy to forget). Neg delegates to Flags.afterSub(0,A,0). Reti/Retn pop pc; Retn also restores iff1 from iff2. Rrd/Rld rotate decimal nibbles between A and (HL); compute S/Z/PV/H=0/N=0/C preserved. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~28 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-2. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-1: IoBus + Cpu.io + Trap B fix + EdOps skeleton
description: Foundation for Phase 2.10. Create IoBus interface + NoIoBus default singleton + Cpu.io field. Apply Trap B fix to FuseSuite.runOne: replace one-shot dispatch with 'while cpu.tStates < startTStates + tStatesToRun' loop. Create empty EdOps fragment + wire into OpTableBuilder. Critical: Trap B test verifies multiple-iteration dispatch works for stub Ops without breaking single-iteration ops. ~6 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-1. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-3: Extended LD pair to/from memory (8 opcodes)
description: Two parameterized classes over RegPair: LdAddrFromPair (ED 43/53/63/73, 20T) writes pair to memory; LdPairFromAddr (ED 4B/5B/6B/7B, 20T) reads from memory into pair. ED 63/6B duplicate main-table 0x22/0x2A but at higher cycle cost (20T vs 16T). No flag changes. Validate pair != AF in init. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~10 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-3. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: Phase 2.10: ED remainder + I/O + Trap B fix
description: Complete the documented Z80 instruction set. ~51 ED-prefixed and main-table-straggler opcodes across ~28 Op classes. Two harness foundations: IoBus interface + Cpu.io field, and the long-deferred Trap B fix to FuseSuite.runOne (loop until tStatesToRun reached so block ops can run multiple iterations within one FUSE case). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-10-ed-remainder-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-10-ed-remainder.md. Phase 2.9 (epic zx80-ux1) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-10.
close_reason: Phase 2.10 complete: ED remainder + I/O + Trap B fix. ~51 opcodes, opcodes count 700/1792, SCORE 0.430, FUSE 487/1356. Z80 documented ISA implementation complete.

title: WU 2.9-4: ResIxd + SetIxd (32 documented (IX+d)/(IY+d) bit ops)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over (IndexReg, n in 0..7). ResIxd at rrr=110 slots in 0x80-0xBF clears bit n; SetIxd at 0xC0-0xFF sets bit n. 8 n × 2 IndexReg × 2 ops = 32 opcodes. 23T (read + modify + write). No flags affected. Both validate n in 0..7. Extend IxCbOps + IxCbOpsTest. After this WU, DDCB/FDCB have 31 documented opcodes each = 62 total. ~13 new tests. Plan WU 2.9-4. Epic: zx80-ux1.

title: WU 2.9-5: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-9

title: WU 2.8-5: JP (IX) and JP (IY) (2 opcodes)

title: WU 2.8-3: LD r,(IX+d) and LD (IX+d),r (28 opcodes)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over (IndexReg, Reg): LdRegFromIxd and LdIxdFromReg. 7 register × 2 index × 2 directions = 28 opcodes. Signed 8-bit displacement at pc+2; effective address (idx + signedD) and 0xFFFF (wraps mod 64K). 19T, R+=2, PC+=3. No flags. Skip rrr=110 (would be (HL) which makes no sense here). Extend IxOps + IxOpsTest. ~12 new tests. Plan WU 2.8-3. Epic: zx80-y7g.

title: WU 2.8-2: Pair-touching IX/IY ops (10 classes, 26 opcodes)

AddIxPair has the wrinkle that srcBits=2 means 'idx itself' (not HL). All 13 opcodes per index = 26 across DD/FD. T-states match HL counterpart + 4. Each Op installs at both DD and FD via IxOps.installPairTouching. ~50 new tests. Plan WU 2.8-2. Epic: zx80-y7g.

Includes straggler LD SP,HL (0xF9 main). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-8-ix-iy-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-8-ix-iy.md. title: WU 2.4-5: RET + RST family (17 opcodes)
description: Three Op classes: Ret (0xC9, 10T, pops pc), RetCc(cond) (8 opcodes 0xC0/C8/D0/D8/E0/E8/F0/F8, 5 not-taken/11 taken), Rst(target) (8 opcodes 0xC7/CF/D7/DF/E7/EF/F7/FF; pushes pc+1; target must be one of 0x00/08/10/18/20/28/30/38; 11T). Mnemonic for RST is e.g. 'RST 18H'. Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~22 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-5. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-4: CALL family (9 opcodes)
description: Two Op classes: CallAbs (0xCD, 17T, pushes pc+3 then jumps), CallAbsCc(cond) (8 opcodes 0xC4/CC/D4/DC/E4/EC/F4/FC, 10 not-taken/17 taken; push happens only on taken branch). Always reads nn even when not taken. Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~13 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-4. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-1: Condition enum + Cpu.push/pop + BranchOps skeleton

Create Cpu.push(mem,value) and Cpu.pop(mem):Int extension methods (pre-decrement push, post-increment pop, little-endian, SP wraps). Create empty BranchOps fragment + wire into OpTableBuilder. ~18 new tests. No Op classes. Plan WU 2.4-1. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-3: JR + DJNZ family (6 opcodes, signed displacement)
description: Three Op classes: JrRel (0x18, 12T), JrRelCc(cond) (4 opcodes 0x20/28/30/38; only NZ/Z/NC/C valid; 7 not-taken/12 taken), Djnz (0x10; decrements B WITHOUT updating flags; 8 not-taken/13 taken). Displacement is signed 8-bit (mem.read().toByte().toInt()). Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~20 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-3. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-2: JP family (10 opcodes)
description: Three Op classes: JpAbs (0xC3, 10T), JpAbsCc(cond) (8 opcodes 0xC2/CA/D2/DA/E2/EA/F2/FA, 10T constant taken/not-taken), JpHl (0xE9, 4T). JpHl sets pc=cpu.hl (NOT memory[HL]). Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~17 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-2. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: Phase 2.4: jumps, calls, returns
description: Implement Z80 control-flow opcodes: JP, JR, DJNZ, CALL, RET, RST and conditional variants. 42 new opcode positions across 11 Op classes. None affect flags. Foundation: Condition enum + Cpu.push/pop helpers. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-4-jumps-calls-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-4-jumps-calls.md. Phase 2.3 (epic zx80-vt6) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-4.

title: WU 2.3-4: AdcHlPair + SbcHlPair (8 ED-prefixed opcodes)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over RegPair, ED-prefixed. 8 opcodes (ADC HL,rr at ED 0x4A/0x5A/0x6A/0x7A; SBC HL,rr at ED 0x42/0x52/0x62/0x72). PC+=2, R+=2 (cpu.bumpR(2)), 15 T-states. Full flag update. Extend AluOps + AluOpsTest. ~12 tests. Plan WU 2.3-4. Epic: zx80-vt6.

title: WU 2.3-3: IncPair + DecPair (8 opcodes, NO flag changes)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over RegPair. 4+4=8 opcodes (INC rr at 0x03/0x13/0x23/0x33; DEC rr at 0x0B/0x1B/0x2B/0x3B). 6 T-states. CRITICAL: 16-bit INC/DEC do NOT affect any flag (unlike 8-bit INC/DEC which preserve only C). Tests set f=0xFF before and assert f=0xFF after. Extend AluOps + AluOpsTest. ~12 tests. Plan WU 2.3-3. Epic: zx80-vt6.

title: WU 2.3-5: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-3

title: WU 2.3-2: AddHlPair (4 opcodes for ADD HL,rr)
description: AddHlPair(src) parameterized over RegPair. 4 opcodes (0x09 BC, 0x19 DE, 0x29 HL, 0x39 SP). 11 T-states. CRITICAL: explicit test asserts S/Z/PV preserved from oldF; ADD HL only touches H/N/C. Extend AluOps + AluOpsTest. ~7 tests. Plan WU 2.3-2. Epic: zx80-vt6.

title: WU 2.3-1: Flags 16-bit helpers (afterAddWord, afterAdcWord, afterSbcWord)
description: Foundation for Phase 2.3. All return AluResult. ~13 new tests covering boundaries (0x0FFF→H, 0x7FFF→V/S, 0xFFFF→C/Z). No Op classes. Plan WU 2.3-1. Epic: zx80-vt6.

title: Phase 2.3: 16-bit arithmetic
description: Implement Z80 16-bit arithmetic on register pairs: ADD HL,rr, INC rr, DEC rr, ADC HL,rr, SBC HL,rr. 20 new opcode positions across 5 Op classes. INC/DEC pair don't touch flags (unique). ADD HL,rr has partial flag update (S/Z/PV preserved from oldF). ADC/SBC HL,rr are ED-prefixed (R+=2, 15 T-states). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-3-arithmetic-16bit-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-3-arithmetic-16bit.md. Phase 2.2 (epic zx80-fg9) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-3.

title: 1qc-gate: FRAMES gate wrong - ROM init takes 132 frames not 50
description:
Investigation of zx80-1qc (ROM stuck at 0x11E6) revealed no buggy Op. The ROM init works correctly but takes ~82 frames to reach EI (correct for ZX Spectrum 48K real hardware ~2s boot), plus 50 more ISR calls = 132 frames before mem.read(0x5C78) reaches 50.

The 50-frame gate is incorrectly specified. Fix: change the FRAMES gate to run 132+ frames and assert mem.read(0x5C78) in [48,52]. Alternatively run 200 frames with assertThat(frames).isGreaterThanOrEqualTo(48).

Investigation artifacts in docs/superpowers/investigations/2026-05-10-zx80-1qc-*.

Hard-stop triggered from zx80-qcs: no single buggy Op found in the 13-instruction loop body. All ops verified correct (FUSE 1356/1356). The gate spec is wrong.
close_reason: Superseded by BootsToBasic end-state check at RUN_FRAMES=500

title: 1qc-fix: Diagnose + fix ROM stuck at 0x11E6 (50-frame FRAMES gate)
description:
Diagnose and fix the ROM init bug that loops at PC=0x11E6. Single task: investigate, find the bug, apply the fix, verify the 50-frame FRAMES gate. Hard-stops on rabbit-holing.

**Spec:** docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-10-zx80-1qc-rom-stuck-investigation-design.md

**Pre-check (MANDATORY first step):**
Add a temporary throwaway test that does:
```kotlin
val machine = Spectrum48k()
machine.reset()
repeat(50) { machine.runFrame() }
assertThat(machine.mem.read(0x5C78)).isBetween(48, 52)
```
Run it. If it PASSES, M1 residuals (LD A,R bumpR ordering) incidentally fixed the ROM init. Revert the test, close this task and the parent zx80-1qc with reason "incidentally fixed by M1 residuals". Done.

If it FAILS, proceed with the investigation.

**Investigation (read full spec for details):**
1. Disassemble ROM 0x11D0-0x1210; save to docs/superpowers/investigations/2026-05-10-zx80-1qc-disasm.txt.
2. Step-trace from real-ROM reset (one-off Kotlin script). Save the tail to docs/superpowers/investigations/2026-05-10-zx80-1qc-trace.txt.
3. Identify the loop body and intended exit condition.
4. For each instruction in the loop, compare expected Z80 behavior (Sean Young's TUZD) vs our implementation. Find the bug.
5. Write a short diagnosis report at docs/superpowers/investigations/2026-05-10-zx80-1qc-diagnosis.md.
6. Apply the fix (smallest possible). Add a TDD unit test that pins the bug behavior.
7. Re-add the 50-frame gate test, verify PASS, revert the throwaway test.
8. Full sweep — FUSE strictly 1356/1356, ZEXDOC clean, programs 5/5, SCORE >= 0.998.

**Hard stop and file follow-up beads if:**
- Loop body has 10+ instructions or unrecognized ops.
- More than one op looks wrong (chain of bugs).
- Fix passes unit test but 50-frame gate still fails.
- Fix breaks FUSE/ZEXDOC/programs.
- Two consecutive op fixes don't unstick the ROM.

**Acceptance:**
- 50-frame FRAMES gate passes (mem.read(0x5C78) in [48, 52]).
- New TDD unit test pins the bug.
- All existing test gates preserved (FUSE 1356/1356, programs 5/5, ZEXDOC clean, SCORE >= 0.998).
- Three artifact files committed in docs/superpowers/investigations/.
- Source change is small and focused on the diagnosed Op.
- Tag optional: m1-rom-init-fix if it feels milestone-worthy.
- Parent zx80-1qc closed.
- Branch pushed.

**Test name reminder:** Kotlin disallows ':' in backtick-quoted test method names. Use commas/dashes.

**When picked up:**
1. Do the pre-check FIRST. Don't skip it.
2. If pre-check fails, follow the investigation procedure in the spec.
3. STOP early per the hard-stop conditions — don't shotgun-fix.
close_reason: Hard stop: no buggy Op found. ROM init works correctly but takes 132 frames (81 frames to EI + 50 ISR calls). The 50-frame FRAMES gate is incorrectly specified - real ZX Spectrum 48K ROM init takes ~2s = ~100 frames. All 13 loop-body ops verified correct (FUSE 1356/1356). Investigation artifacts committed. Follow-up filed: zx80-o41 to correct the gate to 132+ frames.

title: Plan/spec bug: M2.5 SpectrumIoBusTest 'row 1' test uses wrong port (0xFEFD)
description:
In M2.5 plan/spec, the SpectrumIoBusTest test 'read ULA port 0xFEFD returns row 1 ORed with 0xA0' has the wrong port number. Port 0xFEFD has low byte 0xFD with A0=1, so it's a non-ULA port (returns 0xFF). Also the high byte 0xFE selects row 0, not row 1.

To select row 1 via the ULA, the correct port is 0xFDFE (high byte 0xFD = 11111101 selects row 1; low byte 0xFE has A0=0 = ULA).

Files:
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-10-zx80-m2-5-keyboard-matrix-design.md line 396-400
- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-10-zx80-m2-5-keyboard-matrix.md (Task 3 Step 3.1)

Stopped M2.5 execution at Task 3.4 with this failing test. Implementation is correct; the test assertion in the plan is wrong.
close_reason: Fixed: port 0xFEFD → 0xFDFE in spec and plan; A0=0 needed for ULA, high byte 0xFD selects row 1

title: 2 FUSE residuals after Phase G: ed5f (LD A,R N/PV) + ed7d (RETI iff1)
description:
Two tiny FUSE failures remain after Phase G (1354/1356 = 99.85%). Both are 1-line fixes:

(1) ed5f LD A,R: 'af mismatch: 0xF3A1 vs 0xF5A1'. F bits 1 (N) and 2 (PV) differ from expected. Per Phase G subagent: appears to be an interrupt-timing / IFF2 edge case. LD A,R does N=0, PV=IFF2; expected has N=0, PV=1 vs ours N=1, PV=0. Investigate inline flag computation in LdAR.kt — the N flag arithmetic may be off, or Phase F's R-bump-ordering fix may have a small interaction. Quick triage required.

(2) ed7d RETI alias: 'iff1 mismatch'. Per undocumented Z80 docs, RETI on real hardware restores IFF1 from IFF2 identically to RETN. The pre-existing canonical Reti.kt likely doesn't restore IFF1=IFF2; Phase F oversight that only surfaced at the alias slot during Phase G's alias install. Two-line fix in Reti.execute (mirror Retn's iff1 handling).

Score impact: +2 FUSE = 0.966 -> 0.967. To pick up, brainstorm-spec-plan-execute as a small phase or just one-shot fix.
